content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission
April 11, 1991

Michael Schaefer  
Law Offices of Michael Schaefer  
255 S. Grand Avenue, Suite 2006  
Los Angeles, CA 90012

Re: Your Request For Assistance  
Our File No. G-91-116

Dear Mr. Schaefer:

We have received your inquiries concerning your campaign committee's identification number and concerning the interpretation of Elections Code Section 12003.

While it is true that the Fair Political Practices Commission has jurisdiction over some aspects of campaign law, your questions do not concern a subject governed by the Political Reform Act of 1974 ("the Act"), found in Government Code §§ 81000-91015. The Commission's authority is limited to interpretation and enforcement of the Act. Therefore, we cannot provide the assistance you have requested.

As a general rule, please be advised that the Secretary of State's office interprets and enforces the Elections Code. Other agencies which enforce the Elections Code are the district attorneys and the California Attorney General. In your case, I believe the Secretary of State, Elections and Political Reform Division, 1230 J Street, Sacramento, California 95814, (916) 445-0820, is the appropriate state agency to whom you should direct your inquiries.1

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin  
Acting General Counsel

By: Deanne Stone  
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:DS:aa

1 The Elections Code does not contain the statutory provision cited in your letter. Elections Code Sections 29620-29624 address the issue to which you refer in your letter, the corruption of voters, including consideration for voting, bribery at election, and payments for securing votes.
